name: Peano Arithmetic & Gödel — Part 8: Second Incompleteness Theorem
description: Gödel's Second Incompleteness Theorem: If PA is consistent, then PA ⊬ Con(PA). A formal system cannot prove its own consistency. Proof: formalize First Incompleteness; G ↔ ¬Prov(#G) implies Con(PA) → G, so PA ⊢ Con(PA) would give PA ⊢ G, contradicting First Incompleteness.
mermaid:
graph TD
    RefFirst["Ref: First Incompleteness (Part 7)"]
    DefCon["Def: Con(T) = ¬Prov(#⊥̄) = consistency statement"]
    LemD1["Lem: Derivability conditions D1: T ⊢ φ ⇒ T ⊢ Prov(#φ̄)"]
    LemD2["Lem: D2: T ⊢ Prov(#φ→ψ̄) → Prov(#φ̄) → Prov(#ψ̄)"]
    LemD3["Lem: D3: T ⊢ Prov(#φ̄) → Prov(#Prov(#φ̄)̄)"]
    LemFormal["Lem: Formalized First Incompleteness: T ⊢ Con(T) → G"]
    LemGUnprov["Lem: T consistent ⇒ T ⊬ G"]
    ThmSecondIncomp["Thm: Second Incompleteness: T consistent ⇒ T ⊬ Con(T)"]
    CorNoConsProof["Cor: No finitistic proof of consistency of PA within PA"]
    RefFirst --> DefCon
    RefFirst --> LemFormal
    RefFirst --> LemGUnprov
    DefCon --> LemD1
    DefCon --> LemD2
    DefCon --> LemD3
    LemD1 --> LemFormal
    LemD2 --> LemFormal
    LemD3 --> LemFormal
    LemFormal --> ThmSecondIncomp
    LemGUnprov --> ThmSecondIncomp
    ThmSecondIncomp --> CorNoConsProof

    class LemD1,LemD2,LemD3,LemFormal,LemGUnprov lemma
